actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour id 926 maps to script GUID e7dcc90c2e60c4011896c7f21fc1f557 (GvrReticlePointer). Ensure it is enabled, then call its public no-arg API to register as the main pointer so downstream raycasters can detect the pointer.

MonoBehaviour id 926 in scene maps to script GUID e7dcc90c2e60c4011896c7f21fc1f557 (GvrReticlePointer). Ensure the component is enabled, then set this as the active Gvr pointer so downstream raycasters can function.

MonoBehaviour id 915 on ddcontroller maps to script GUID aa952cbcc0eb13d4ca558b6da550ff55 (class GvrControllerVisual) exactly matching the provided source code. Its serialized fields (touchPoint fileID 109; material_idle/app/system/touchpad; touchTransparent/touchOpaque) align with the scene meta. This component has no UnityEvents (m_Calls) and exposes no public no-arg methods; its logic runs via GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate and Unity lifecycle (Awake/Start/OnDestroy) under UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR && (UNITY_ANDROID || UNITY_EDITOR). ddcontroller has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger, so there are no manual Trigger-able events from the scene side. To achieve code coverage we need the GameObject/script providing GvrArmModel.Instance and a way to simulate GvrController state (ClickButton, AppButton, Recentering, IsTouching, TouchPos).
